title: 1.1: Tests that Parse returns the expected ETag for a valid unquoted hex string
1.2: Tests that Parse strips surrounding double quotes from quoted ETag strings
1.3: Tests that Parse handles multipart ETags with part-number suffix
1.4: Tests that Parse returns an error for malformed ETag strings
1.5: Tests that Equal returns true when comparing two identical ETag values
1.6: Tests that Equal treats quoted and unquoted versions of the same ETag as equal
1.7: Tests that Equal returns false when comparing different ETag values
1.8: Tests that IsEncrypted returns true for at least one known encrypted ETag input
1.9: Tests that IsEncrypted returns false for standard non-encrypted ETags
1.10: Tests that IsEncrypted returns false for multipart ETags
1.11: Tests that Format returns the ETag unchanged for non-encrypted ETags
1.12: Tests that Format preserves the part-number suffix for multipart ETags
1.13: Tests that Format produces the expected AWS S3-compatible string for at least one encrypted ETag input
1.15: Tests that Parse rejects multipart-like ETags containing multiple dashes in the suffix (e.g., '-2-1')
2.1: Places all new ETag-related tests in a *_test.go file under internal/etag (e.g., internal/etag/etag_test.go)
3.1: All tests use Go's standard testing package with a *testing.T parameter
3.2: All test function names follow the Go Test* naming convention
3.3: When multiple cases are tested, tests use table-driven loops or subtests (t.Run) for organization
3.4: Tests report failures using t.Fatalf, t.Errorf, or related testing.T methods
4.1: Parse tests construct at least one expected ETag value for a successful-parse case without calling Parse (to avoid circular expectations)
4.2: Format tests assert AWS compatibility using the formatted ETag's String() output compared to an expected string
4.3: IsEncrypted tests use ETag values produced by Parse from ETag strings (not synthetic byte-slice construction like ETag(make([]byte, N)))
4.4: Tests perform Parse calls inside test functions (not during package-level initialization)
